test_now.name: Insider Buying in Small-Caps
test_now.signal_rule: Identify stocks where insiders (directors, officers, 10%+ holders) make net purchases. Universe: small-cap equities (smallest 50% by market cap). Long-only equal-weight portfolio; quarterly rebalancing based on insider transaction reports.
test_now.claimed: 7.4% abnormal 12-month return for small-cap insider buys; 10% spread (buys vs. sells)
test_now.primitive: Reuse alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (full battery) and clone the event-driven scaffold alpca/backtest/ear_pead.py + scripts/test_ear_pead.py into scripts/test_insider_smallcap.py (signal->event->hold->OOS->cost->DSR identical structure). Reuse scripts/download_data.py (SIP daily bars) and scripts/download_fundamentals_edgar.py (shares-outstanding for PIT market-cap). NEW CODE only for: (a) AV INSIDER_TRANSACTIONS fetcher alpca/data/insider.py (mirror alpca/data/earnings.py AV pattern), (b) small-cap PIT universe classifier, (c) net-insider-buy quarterly signal + overlapping-cohort 12mo-hold portfolio builder.
test_now.test_plan:
REUSE the event-driven PEAD scaffold (alpca/backtest/ear_pead.py + scripts/test_ear_pead.py) and the full eval battery in alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (B&H, beta_alpha, deflated_sharpe_ratio, segment_sharpes, sharpe_pvalue, OOS split). Only the signal+universe layer is new code.

DATA / CONSTRUCTION:
1. Universe: build a ~120-symbol small-cap basket fetchable via Alpaca SIP (constituents/proxies of IWM/IJR; liquid sub-$3B names). Classify "small-cap = smallest 50%" PIT each quarter using shares-outstanding from EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (already fetched by scripts/download_fundamentals_edgar.py, keyed on `filed` = no lookahead) times price. Keep a survivorship-clean list incl. delisted names where AV/SIP still serve history.
2. Bars: scripts/download_data.py --symbols <basket> --timeframe 1day --years 10 (2016-2026 SIP daily). Benchmark = IWM (small-cap B&H), plus SPY for beta.
3. Insider signal: AV INSIDER_TRANSACTIONS (CONFIRMED live, 2012- history, ~4k recs/sym). Per name per quarter NET insider BUYS = sum(A shares*price) minus sells, restricted to directors/officers/10%+ via executive_title. Signal = net-buy > 0 (paper: only buys predictive). LOOKAHEAD CONTROL (critical): AV gives only transaction_date, NOT Form 4 filing date -- lag every signal +2 business days (statutory Form 4 deadline) before tradeable; robustness pass at +5.
4. Portfolio: long-only equal-weight names with net insider buys, quarterly rebalance, 12mo hold (overlapping cohorts -> daily equity curve), 2bps/leg + 3-5bps small-cap slippage on rebalance turnover only.

EXACT CONTROLS:
- vs B&H: must beat IWM (small-cap index), not just SPY.
- beta-decomposition: beta_alpha() vs IWM AND SPY; annualized alpha + IR. Edge must be alpha not small-cap beta.
- out-of-sample: 70/30 chronological split; IS and OOS Sharpe both positive and comparable.
- out-of-regime: segment by year (2016-19 / 2020 covid / 2022 bear / 2023-26); positive in >=4 buckets (the multi-regime test that killed Case 21 SI-tilt).
- out-of-universe (the EAR-PEAD killer): TRAIN-60 / disjoint HOLDOUT-60 by symbol; fit thresholds on TRAIN, confirm on HOLDOUT. Make-or-break gate.
- realistic cost/borrow: long-only so NO borrow (genuine advantage vs PEAD/SI-tilt). Small-cap 3-5bps spread + 2bps quarterly turnover.
- DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio with n_trials counting every threshold/lag/title-filter variant tried (>=20).
- placebo: shuffle insider-buy labels across names within each quarter -> placebo Sharpe ~0.
test_now.predicted_kill: It's a 12-month-hold, long-only, small-cap-ONLY basket -> the beta-decomposition control shows it is just small-cap (IWM/SP600) factor beta, not alpha. Like every single-asset/long-only family already rejected here, residual alpha vs IWM B&H collapses to ~0 once small-cap beta is stripped -- the paper's '7.4% abnormal' is a CAPM residual vs the broad market, erased by our hard small-cap-B&H bar plus the 2016-2026 small-cap-beta tailwind. Secondary: AV gives only transaction_date (no Form 4 filing date), so the lookahead-safe +2d lag plus thin small-cap fills erode any thin residual; tertiary: the out-of-universe holdout (the EAR-PEAD killer) likely fails since the per-name signal is sparse and noisy.
test_now.objection: Survivorship/PIT-classification trap, not plain beta. The test plan defuses the obvious "it's just small-cap beta" worry by gating against IWM (small-cap index) via beta_alpha plus the fresh-symbol holdout. The real danger is the universe: a small-cap basket built today and backfilled silently drops names that delisted to zero — precisely the failed small-caps where insider buying is a desperation/dilution-funding signal — which inflates returns upward (this exact survivorship hazard is why the plan must source delisted names and classify smallest-50% PIT from EDGAR filed-date shares-outstanding). Secondary: every prior fundamental on this repo failed the fresh-symbol holdout EXCEPT value (Case 24), and value still came in 'real but too thin' (~0.14 Sharpe, DSR ~0.3, heavily regime-timed) — so the realistic ceiling is real-but-thin, below the ~0.2 cost-survival floor. Tertiary data hazards (both already flagged in the plan): AV gives only transaction_date not Form 4 filing date (needs +2 biz-day lag, confirmed empirically), and many 'A' acquisition records carry empty share_price (option/grant noise that must be filtered to priced open-market buys).

test_now.name: Insider Trading Opportunistic Buy Signal
test_now.signal_rule: Identify opportunistic insider purchases (exclude routine vesting); aggregate by stock; long top quintile by insider purchase intensity; short bottom quintile; monthly rebalance; hold 1-3 months
test_now.claimed: +0.57% excess S&P 500 return next month per 1-std aggregate insider buys; 6%+ annual abnormal return for insider buy-heavy stocks
test_now.primitive: REUSE: alpca/backtest/factor.py::backtest_factor (monthly dollar-neutral quintile L/S engine) + factor.py::_fundamental_step (EDGAR filed-date-lagged step signal builder) for a new insider_intensity_signal(); alpca/backtest/pead.py adverse_borrow model for the short-leg borrow stress; alpca/backtest/evaluation.py full battery (buy_and_hold, beta_alpha, information_ratio, oos, segment_sharpes, deflated_sharpe_ratio, sharpe_tstat); scripts/test_ear_pead_holdout.py MODE-B fresh-symbol disjoint-partition harness. NEW CODE: only one piece — a Form 4 fetcher (scripts/download_insider_form4.py, cloned from download_fundamentals_edgar.py: companyfacts->submissions, parse ownership Form 4, P vs A/M classification, filed-date keying) plus a thin test_insider_form4.py wiring the signal into backtest_factor + the controls.
test_now.test_plan:
DATA & CONSTRUCTION:
1) Universe: ~120-180 US single-name equities already SIP-cached in data cache (the same 195-symbol universe the EDGAR/AV jobs walk). Insider effects are documented strongest in small/microcaps, so DELIBERATELY include the lower-liquidity / smaller-cap names already fetchable on Alpaca SIP (avoid an all-mega-cap universe, which is the regime where this anomaly is weakest -> would self-kill on a non-representative panel).
2) Signal source = SEC EDGAR Form 4 (the actual disclosure venue; the paper's "FINRA insider" framing is really Form 4). NEW fetcher modeled on scripts/download_fundamentals_edgar.py: hit data.sec.gov submissions/CIK#######.json + the Form 4 XBRL/ownership feed, parse transactionCode='P' (open-market PURCHASE) vs 'A'/'M' (vesting/award/option-exercise = ROUTINE, EXCLUDE), keep acquisition shares*price = dollar buy. CRITICAL no-lookahead: key every transaction off the FILING acceptance datetime (filed within 2 business days of the trade), never the transaction date. Cache per symbol as [{filed_epoch, code, dollars, role(officer/director/10pct)}].
3) Build signal_fn 'insider_intensity_signal(form4_by_sym)' via the EXISTING alpca/backtest/factor.py::_fundamental_step pattern (it already lags fundamentals to the EDGAR 'filed' date and forward-fills) -> per name, trailing-3-month sum of OPPORTUNISTIC purchase dollars normalized by market cap (or std-of-net-buys, matching the paper's '+0.57%/1-std' metric). 'Opportunistic' filter = exclude routine/scheduled (10b5-1-ish, awards, exercises); paper notes recent >10% price-move purchases carry the most signal, so add a variant that up-weights buys following a recent drawdown.
4) Engine = alpca/backtest/factor.py::backtest_factor (already does monthly dollar-neutral quintile L/S with turnover cost): top_frac=0.2, rebalance_days=21, long_high=True (long top insider-buy quintile, short bottom), cost_bps=2.0/leg, hold 1-3mo emerges naturally from 21d rebal + slow signal.

EXACT CONTROLS (the full battery, all already in alpca/backtest/evaluation.py):
- vs BUY-AND-HOLD: buy_and_hold() on SPY over the identical window; require strat beats B&H net.
- BETA-DECOMPOSITION: beta_alpha(strat_eq, SPY_eq, 252) -> the verdict is on ALPHA (annualized) + information_ratio, NOT raw return. Long-quintile insider stocks are small/value-tilted = beta+size, so report the long-leg, short-leg, and dollar-neutral combo SEPARATELY (history: PEAD/EAR/SI-tilt 'edges' were all carried by a beta-y long leg).
- OUT-OF-SAMPLE: oos() 70/30 chronological split; require OOS Sharpe ~ IS Sharpe (no decay).
- OUT-OF-REGIME: segment by year-buckets across 2016-2026 SIP (10.5yr); require positive in a MAJORITY of regimes and survive a bear (2018Q4, 2020, 2022) — this is the multi-regime test that killed SI-tilt (DSR 0.98 1yr artifact).
- FRESH-SYMBOL HOLDOUT (the decisive one, per memory): split universe into disjoint TRAIN vs HOLDOUT symbol sets using the scripts/test_ear_pead_holdout.py MODE-B partition; require HOLDOUT Sharpe holds (TRAIN-only edge = overfit, retract). This is the test that killed EAR-PEAD.
- REALISTIC COST/BORROW: 2bps/leg already in engine; for the SHORT leg apply the pead.py adverse_borrow model {base,special,sat,no_locate} — the bottom insider-buy quintile is disproportionately small/HTB/no-locate names, so price the short leg with the saturating adverse-selection borrow ramp and DROP no-locate names. Report combo Sharpe BEFORE and AFTER realistic borrow.
- DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio(equity, n_trials, sharpe_variance) with n_trials counting the variants tried (intensity normalization, opportunistic-filter cutoffs, top_frac, rebal cadence, recent-move up-weight) — require DSR > 0.95 to call it validated.
- PLACEBO: shuffle-placebo (randomize Form 4 filed dates within each name) -> edge must vanish; confirms it's the insider timing, not panel structure.

PASS BAR: net-of-cost-and-adverse-borrow dollar-neutral Sharpe positive, beats B&H on alpha, IS~OOS, positive across regimes, DSR>0.95, AND survives the fresh-symbol holdout. Anything that only passes in-sample/in-universe = REJECT (paid that lesson twice).
test_now.predicted_kill: The SHORT leg dies on adverse-selection borrow + the LONG leg is small-cap/value BETA, not alpha. Bottom insider-buy quintile names are disproportionately small/illiquid/HTB — exactly the names that go special/no-locate, so the short leg is unfundable on Alpaca's price-taker, no-rebate, adverse-borrow venue (the identical wall that killed surprise-PEAD and the SI-tilt). Once the short is neutered, the dollar-neutral combo collapses to the long leg, which beta_alpha will show is carried by size/value exposure (~zero net alpha) — and even if a residual survives in-sample, the documented small/microcap concentration plus monthly rebalance makes it a prime fresh-symbol-holdout failure (the EAR-PEAD pattern: in-universe pass, out-of-universe collapse).
test_now.objection: The short leg dies on the same adverse-selection borrow wall that killed PEAD/SI-tilt three times (PEAD short leg −0.47 standalone; DSR collapsed 0.92→0.58 under realistic special/no-locate borrow). The bottom insider-buy quintile is disproportionately small/illiquid/HTB — exactly the names that go special or no-locate on Alpaca's price-taker, no-rebate venue, so the short is unfundable. Once the short is neutered, the dollar-neutral combo collapses to a long leg that beta_alpha will likely expose as size/value BETA, not alpha. And the documented small/microcap concentration + monthly rebal makes it a prime fresh-symbol-holdout casualty (the EAR-PEAD −0.52 / Accruals −0.45→−0.76 pattern: in-universe pass, out-of-universe collapse).

test_now.name: Supply Chain Customer Momentum Lead-Lag
test_now.signal_rule: Identify customer-supplier relationships via SEC 10-K filings (>=10% revenue); rank customer returns vs supplier returns 1-12 months prior; long suppliers of positive-return customers, short suppliers of negative-return customers; rebalance quarterly
test_now.claimed: 56 bps monthly abnormal return (supplier momentum); international evidence across 22 countries
test_now.primitive: Reuse: alpca/backtest/cross_sectional.py (backtest_cross_sectional_momentum as the L/S dollar-neutral trading+turnover-cost engine), alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (buy_and_hold, beta_alpha, sharpe_of, probabilistic_sharpe_ratio, deflated_sharpe_ratio, walk-forward/regime helpers), the SHUFFLE-PLACEBO pattern from scripts/test_lead_lag.py (lead_lag.py shuffle_leaders flag), adverse-selection borrow ramp from the PEAD/short-interest cases, scripts/download_data.py for bars. NEW CODE: (a) a supply_chain_links module that builds the PIT customer->supplier graph and emits per-quarter supplier signals = link-weighted lagged customer returns; (b) a thin scripts/test_supply_chain_leadlag.py wiring that signal into the cross_sectional engine + running the full control battery incl. link-randomized placebo and beta-vs-generic-XS-momentum.
test_now.test_plan:
PILOT-FIRST, single falsifying battery. Reuse the existing harness; the ONLY new code is the customer->supplier LINK GRAPH (no such data exists in the repo or in EDGAR companyfacts XBRL — it lives in unstructured 10-K text under ASC 280 'major customers >=10% of revenue').

(1) DATA/CONSTRUCTION. Hand-build a PIT link table of ~15-25 well-documented disclosed customer->supplier edges (e.g. AAPL<-its disclosed suppliers Skyworks/Qorvo/etc; TSMC<-... ; Walmart/Target/Costco<-staple suppliers; GM/Ford<-auto-parts; major-customer pairs both Alpaca-SIP-fetchable as US-listed). Pull 10.5yr daily SIP bars for every customer and supplier ticker. Signal: each quarter, compute each CUSTOMER's trailing 1-12mo return; the supplier's signal = its customer's lagged return (mean over its customers, link-weighted by disclosed % when available). Cross-sectionally demean across suppliers -> dollar-neutral long suppliers-of-up-customers / short suppliers-of-down-customers. Hold 3mo (63 bars), rebalance quarterly via overlapping tranches. NO LOOK-AHEAD: customer return window ends >=1 trading day before the supplier position is entered; links keyed off the 10-K filed date, never fiscal-year-end.

(2) THE EXACT CONTROLS (all already in alpca/backtest/evaluation.py):
  - vs BUY-AND-HOLD: buy_and_hold(SPY) + a long-only-suppliers basket; the L/S spread must beat both risk-adjusted.
  - BETA DECOMPOSITION: beta_alpha(strat_eq, SPY_eq, 252) -> require positive, significant ANNUALIZED ALPHA, not beta. Also regress on a generic cross-sectional-momentum factor (backtest_cross_sectional_momentum on the SAME supplier universe with RANDOM/no links) to prove the edge is the LINK, not plain large-cap XS-momentum.
  - SHUFFLE PLACEBO (decisive, the Case-19 killer): re-run with customer->supplier links assigned at RANDOM (preserve degree distribution). Real must beat placebo by a margin outside noise (Case 19 bar: +0.1 Sharpe is NOT enough).
  - OUT-OF-SAMPLE / WALK-FORWARD: links + sign fit on 2016-2021 train, traded forward on 2022-2026 held-out windows, rolled (quarterly cadence => ~15-20 OOS quarters).
  - OUT-OF-REGIME: per-calendar-year Sharpe 2016-2026; require positive in MOST years incl. 2018/2022 drawdowns (not concentrated in one COVID-supply-shock window 2020-21).
  - REALISTIC COST/BORROW: 2bps/leg taker on each quarterly rebalance (low turnover helps here, ~1/3 book/quarter); SHORT leg under adverse-selection borrow ramp (worst-momentum suppliers go special/no-locate) -> also test SPY-index-hedged short variant (Case 18 trick) since single-name supplier shorts are borrow-fragile.
  - DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio deflated by the project's TRUE search breadth (hundreds of configs across all cases, not the ~5 of this sweep); require DSR > 0.9 to even consider, > 0.95 to ship.
VERDICT GATE: ship only if real > placebo by a clear margin AND positive alpha vs both SPY and the link-randomized XS-momentum control AND OOS~=IS AND positive most years AND survives 2bps + adverse borrow AND DSR>0.95. Pilot first; only expand the link graph (parse 10-K text via EDGAR full-text search for 'major customers'/Item 1 concentration) if the pilot survives placebo+beta.
test_now.predicted_kill: SHUFFLE PLACEBO + beta/sector confound: once dollar-neutral with honest quarterly cost, the disclosed-link signal will be statistically indistinguishable from a randomized-link or plain large-cap cross-sectional-momentum control (exactly Case 19's data-driven lead-lag outcome, +0.11 over placebo = noise). The disclosed >=10% links are concentrated in a few mega-cap customers (AAPL/Walmart/auto OEMs), so the 'network' signal collapses into shared sector/beta loadings, not idiosyncratic information diffusion. Secondary kill: the 56bps/mo academic alpha was a small/mid-cap, pre-2010, supplier-inattention effect that has decayed and does not survive on the liquid, well-covered US large caps we can actually trade — and the short leg is borrow-fragile.
test_now.objection: Dollar-neutral with honest quarterly cost, the disclosed customer->supplier signal will likely be statistically indistinguishable from BOTH (a) a degree-preserving link-randomized shuffle placebo and (b) plain large-cap cross-sectional momentum on the identical universe — because disclosed >=10%-revenue links concentrate in a handful of mega-cap customers (AAPL / Walmart-Target-Costco / auto OEMs), so the 'network' collapses into shared sector/beta loadings rather than idiosyncratic information diffusion. This is the exact data-driven outcome of Case 19 (real -1.02 vs placebo -1.14, +0.11 = noise; dies at 1bp). Secondary kill: the 56bps/mo academic alpha was a pre-2010 small/mid-cap supplier-INATTENTION effect that decays on the liquid, well-covered US large caps we can actually trade, and the short supplier leg is borrow-fragile (worst-momentum names go special/no-locate).

test_now.name: Option Expiration Week Effect (SPY long 3rd Friday week)
test_now.signal_rule: Monitor 3rd Friday of month (US stock options expiration). If current Monday is in expiration week OR expiration date is ≤5 days away, long SPY (S&P 100 index proxy). Otherwise, hold cash. Exit at expiration.
test_now.primitive: REUSE alpca/backtest/seasonality.py (backtest_seasonal + position-vector pattern) and alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (buy_and_hold, beta_alpha, deflated_sharpe_ratio, sharpe_pvalue/tstat, segment_sharpes, oos split) — mirror scripts/test_seasonality.py. NEW CODE = just one function expiration_week_position() + a random-week placebo loop (~40 lines total).
test_now.test_plan:
REUSE the seasonality primitive end-to-end (mirror scripts/test_seasonality.py). New code = ONE position-builder added to alpca/backtest/seasonality.py: `expiration_week_position(bars, days_window=5)` returning a 0/1 vector = 1.0 on the trading days of the week containing the 3rd Friday of each month (Mon-Fri of OpEx week; also test the published variant "Mon of OpEx week through expiration Friday" and a "≤5 days to 3rd Friday" variant). Computing the 3rd Friday from each bar's UTC timestamp is pure-calendar (no options data needed) — exactly why this is Alpaca-feasible. Then run the SAME control battery already wired in evaluation.py:

DATA: 10.5yr daily SIP bars (2016-2026, adjusted) for SPY (primary). Also fetch QQQ, IWM, DIA as cross-instrument replications (a real OpEx effect should appear across index ETFs, not just SPY). One fetch_alpaca_bars call each, cached to data/cache/*.jsonl. No external drive, no options, no shorts, long-only — fully on-venue.

CONSTRUCTION: position vector held into next day's close-to-close return; cost_bps=2.0/leg via backtest_seasonal (turnover is ~24 trades/yr, 2 legs per OpEx cycle, so cost is negligible — this is NOT a cost-wall strategy). Exposure will be ~20-25% of days.

CONTROLS (all exist as primitives):
1. vs BUY-AND-HOLD: buy_and_hold(SPY) is the benchmark. Report excess return AND — critically — EXPOSURE-MATCHED B&H: scale B&H to the same ~22% time-in-market (i.e., does OpEx week beat a RANDOM 22%-exposure window?). This is the decisive comparison, not raw return.
2. BETA-DECOMPOSITION: beta_alpha(strat_eq, SPY_eq, 252) — the test is whether annualized ALPHA > 0 and the info_ratio is meaningful. A long-only calendar slice of SPY will have beta≈0.22 and near-zero true alpha if there's no real effect.
3. RANDOM-WEEK PLACEBO (the key falsifier): build 1,000 position vectors each picking a RANDOM 5-day window per month (same count/exposure as OpEx week) and compute the Sharpe/return distribution; OpEx week must sit in the top tail (empirical p<0.05) of that null. This isolates "is THIS week special" from "any long week in a bull market makes money."
4. OUT-OF-SAMPLE: 70/30 chronological split via the oos() helper — IS Sharpe vs OOS Sharpe must both be positive and similar.
5. OUT-OF-REGIME: segment by year (segment_sharpes / per-year breakdown) — the published effect is 2007-2018; our window is 2016-2026, so test whether it holds in 2016-2019, 2020-2022 (COVID/bear), 2023-2026 separately. A real seasonal must be positive in a majority of regimes, not carried by 2020-2021.
6. DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio(equity, n_trials, sharpe_variance) — set n_trials to honestly count the variant search (window definitions × 4 ETFs × entry/exit variants ≈ 30-50 trials) so the in-sample Sharpe is deflated for multiple testing. Require DSR>0.95 to call it real.
7. COST/BORROW: long-only ETF, so 2bps/leg only; no borrow/adverse-selection applies (no shorts). Note no maker rebates needed (low turnover).
test_now.predicted_kill: RANDOM-WEEK PLACEBO + EXPOSURE-MATCHED BETA: the strategy is long SPY only ~22% of days in a market that tripled 2016-2026, so it will show positive raw return AND positive raw Sharpe purely from a fractional slice of the bull-market beta — exactly the trap that killed momentum (BETA NOT ALPHA) in our prior work. OpEx week will fail to beat a random same-exposure 5-day window (placebo p>0.05) and its beta-decomposed alpha will be ~0. The published 2007-2018 effect is also documented to have weakened/inverted post-2010, so it will likely fail out-of-regime even if a thin in-sample edge appears. Expect: gross Sharpe looks fine, alpha≈0, placebo non-significant, DSR<0.5 → REJECT as repackaged beta.
test_now.objection: It is a long-only ~22%-exposure slice of SPY in a market that tripled 2016-2026, so it will show positive raw return/Sharpe purely from harvesting a fractional slice of bull-market BETA — the identical trap that killed momentum and demoted turn-of-month/pre-FOMC to "not standalone alpha" in this repo. Decisive falsifiers: (1) it should NOT beat an exposure-matched random 5-day-window-per-month placebo (expect p>0.05), and (2) beta-decomposed alpha should be ~0. The economic story is also weak/backwards (OpEx = dealer gamma roll-off → more realized vol, not a clean directional long premium), the source publishes zero backtest metrics, and the documented effect weakened/inverted post-2010 — our entire 2016-2026 window sits in the decayed regime, so it likely also fails the per-year out-of-regime split. Expected outcome: gross Sharpe looks fine, alpha≈0, random-week placebo non-significant, DSR<0.5 → REJECT as repackaged beta.

test_now.name: Bond ETF duration rotation (3-tier median rebalance)
test_now.signal_rule: Rank 6 Treasury/bond ETFs (VGSH=short, VGIT=intermediate, VGLT=long, or BND equivalents) by 1-month lagged return. Divide into 3 equal tiers; hold middle tier (median performers). Rebalance monthly.
test_now.claimed: CAGR 3.79%, grows $100 to $199.90 by end-2025 vs. 2.17% passive benchmark
test_now.primitive: Reuse: scripts/download_data.py (fetch bars to local cache), alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (buy_and_hold, beta_alpha, information_ratio, segment_sharpes, deflated_sharpe_ratio, sharpe_of, max_drawdown_of). New code: ~40-line scripts/test_bond_rotation.py implementing the rank→3-tier→hold-MIDDLE long-only equal-weight monthly engine (backtest_factor does L/S tails, not a long-only median tier, so it cannot be reused directly — but it's the structural template, mirror its no-lookahead signal[t-1] lag and 21d rebalance loop).
test_now.test_plan:
DATA: Fetch 10.5yr daily SIP bars (2016-01 to 2026-06) for the duration ladder + benchmarks via `scripts/download_data.py --symbols VGSH,VGIT,VGLT,SHY,IEF,TLT,BND,AGG --timeframe 1day --years 11` writing JSONL to local `data/cache_bondrot` (NO external drive). Core universe = the 6-ETF duration ladder the paper uses (VGSH/VGIT/VGLT + 3 BND-family equivalents); benchmarks = BND/AGG (passive aggregate), plus IEF & VGIT alone (the median-duration B&H that the rotation will most resemble). Pull AlphaVantage T-bill/3mo yield only as a cash-leg reference (not strictly needed for long-only).

CONSTRUCTION (new code, ~40 lines — `scripts/test_bond_rotation.py`): build a price matrix from the cache; each month-end (rebalance_days=21), rank the N ETFs by 1-month (21d) lagged return KNOWN AS OF rebalance day (no-lookahead, identical lag convention to `backtest_factor` line 88 `signal[t-1]`); split into 3 equal tiers; hold the MIDDLE tier equal-weight, LONG-ONLY, until next rebalance; apply 2bps/leg on turnover. NOTE: `backtest_factor` cannot be reused as-is (it longs the top tier / shorts the bottom — tails, not the median, and is dollar-neutral), so the tier-selector + long-only equity-curve accrual is the new piece; everything downstream reuses primitives.

THE EXACT CONTROLS (all from `alpca/backtest/evaluation.py`):
1) vs BUY-AND-HOLD: `buy_and_hold()` on BND, AGG, and median-duration IEF/VGIT. Pass bar = strategy beats the BEST single passive bond ETF net of cost, not just the cap-weighted aggregate (paper's 2.17% benchmark is weak; the honest hurdle is "hold IEF and do nothing").
2) BETA-DECOMPOSITION: `beta_alpha(strat_eq, bench_eq, ppy=252)` against (a) BND and (b) a duration factor proxy = TLT-minus-SHY long-short return. If beta≈1 on BND and annualized alpha≈0 (or <2bps/day t<2), it is duration beta, not edge. Also `information_ratio()` vs BND.
3) OUT-OF-SAMPLE / OUT-OF-REGIME: split 2016-2021 (ZIRP/QE, falling-flat rates) vs 2022-2026 (hiking shock + 2022 bond crash + 2023-24 cuts). Report Sharpe per regime via `segment_sharpes(eq, 252, k=4)` AND an explicit 2-regime split. A real edge must survive BOTH the ZIRP era and the 2022 rate-shock era with the SAME sign.
4) OUT-OF-UNIVERSE proxy: re-run on a disjoint ladder (IEF/TLT/SHY/GOVT/SCHO/SPTL) the rule never saw, since 6 tickers can't be symbol-split — this is the closest fresh-symbol holdout available for a tiny fixed universe.
5) REALISTIC COST: 2bps/leg on monthly turnover (long-only, so low turnover, ~12 rebals/yr; cost is NOT the killer here, unlike intraday cases). No borrow needed (long-only). Report net AND gross.
6) DSR: `deflated_sharpe_ratio(eq, n_trials, sharpe_variance=1e-4)` with n_trials >= the # of (tier-choice × lookback × rebalance-cadence) configs implicitly searched (median vs top vs bottom = 3, lookback 1/3/6mo = 3, monthly/quarterly = 2 → n_trials≈18-25). Require DSR>0.95 AND positive alpha in BOTH regimes AND beat best-B&H net. Tiny-universe variance correction: bootstrap-resample the 21d return ranks to confirm the median-beats-tails claim isn't a 2-name fluke.

VERDICT RULE: ship only if net-of-cost it beats hold-IEF, alpha-vs-BND is positive with t>2 in BOTH regimes, and DSR>0.95. Otherwise REJECT as duration beta.
test_now.predicted_kill: vs-B&H + beta-decomposition: the ~3.79% CAGR is just intermediate-duration bond beta. All 6 ETFs share one dominant common factor (the level/slope of the Treasury curve), so the median-of-6 selection is a ~2-name basket whose return is ~95% explained by holding a mid-duration ETF (IEF/BND) — buy-and-hold of BND/IEF captures it for free with zero turnover, leaving alpha≈0. The 2022 rate-shock regime is the specific killer: a 1-month-lagged median-reversion rule has no defense against a monotonic 2022 selloff, so per-regime alpha flips sign (works in 2016-21 falling rates, breaks in 2022 hiking) and fails the out-of-regime control even before DSR.
test_now.objection: The 6 Treasury ETFs are 6 points on ONE yield curve (pairwise corr ~0.85-0.98, ~1.5 effective dimensions), so "rank-6, hold-the-median-2 monthly" mechanically reduces to "hold something near intermediate duration nearly always." The 3.79% CAGR is intermediate-Treasury BETA you capture for free by holding IEF/VGIT and doing nothing; alpha-vs-BND ≈ 0. The decisive kill is the out-of-regime split: the paper's 2007-2025 window is dominated by the falling-rate bond bull, and a 1-month-LAGGED reversion rule has zero defense against the 2022 monotonic rate shock (worst Treasury year on record). Per-regime alpha flips sign (works 2016-21 falling rates, breaks 2022 hiking) and fails the 2-regime control before DSR even runs. The "median beats both winners AND losers" framing is the signature of an in-sample fit on a tiny universe over a single macro regime, not a discovery.

test_now.name: Higher-Moment Tail Risk Hedging (Skewness Optimization)
test_now.direction: dynamic defensive tilt based on tail characteristics
test_now.signal_rule: For a baseline equity long portfolio (SPY): measure 60-day rolling skewness and kurtosis. When skewness < -0.3 and kurtosis > 4 (fat negative tail), reduce equity exposure by 20-30% and hold cash or buy VIX-sensitive defensive assets (long IVV, short-vol hedge off, or hold TLT). Entry: drawdown > 10% with negative skew; Exit: skewness > -0.1 for 20 days.
data_needs: daily OHLC bars for SPY (60+ days rolling)
test_now.claimed: reduces maximum drawdown 20-40% vs. unhedged equity during tail events; Sortino ratio improvement
test_now.primitive: REUSE almost everything: alpca/backtest/seasonality.py::backtest_seasonal (overlay engine — takes arbitrary daily position series + cost_bps); alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (_skew_kurt for rolling moments, evaluate() for vs-B&H+beta/alpha+OOS+segment-regime+significance, deflated_sharpe_ratio + probabilistic_sharpe_ratio for DSR); per-calendar-year loop + DSR-vs-trials recipe lifted from scripts/audit_overfit.py; tail-stress injection from scripts/test_shortvol_tail_stress.py (only if vol-ETF variant). NEW CODE = a single thin script (scripts/test_skew_overlay.py, ~120 lines, mirror test_seasonality.py): (1) compute rolling-skew/kurt -> weekly weight series with the drawdown-gate + 20d hysteresis, (2) the static-beta PLACEBO comparison curve (the load-bearing new control), (3) optional w*SPY+(1-w)*TLT blend with turnover accounting.
test_now.test_plan:
CONSTRUCTION (single-benchmark conditional-weight overlay, weekly rebalance):
- Universe: SPY (core long). Hedge assets fetched same way: TLT (defensive), and optionally UVXY/SVXY as a small vol sleeve. Keep the core comparison SPY-vs-cash/TLT; treat vol-ETFs as an add-on scenario, not the headline (path-dependent decay + the tail-stress veto below).
- Signal: each day compute 60-day ROLLING skewness and kurtosis of SPY daily returns using the exact estimators already in alpca/backtest/evaluation.py (_skew_kurt). Use a trailing window ending at t-1 (NO lookahead). Position weight w_t in {0.7, 0.8, 1.0}: w=0.7 (reduce 30%) when skew<-0.3 AND kurt>4 (note kurt here is non-excess, normal=3, so >4 is mild — verify the harbourfront convention; if they mean excess>4 i.e. raw>7, test BOTH thresholds as the a-priori grid, do not tune to the best). Apply the drawdown-gate (enter defensive only when rolling drawdown> -10% AND neg skew) and the 20-day skew>-0.1 exit hysteresis exactly as written. Park the de-risked 20-30% in cash (rf=0) for the headline run; TLT-parked and vol-sleeve as separate scenarios. Rebalance weekly (Friday), hold the weight through the week.
- Engine: REUSE alpca/backtest/seasonality.py::backtest_seasonal(bars, position, cost_bps=2.0) — it already takes an arbitrary continuous daily position series, multiplies p*r, and charges cost_bps on |turnover|. The skew-overlay is structurally identical to the seasonality overlays. For the multi-asset (TLT/vol) variant write a thin 2-line blend on top (w*SPY_ret + (1-w)*hedge_ret) reusing the same turnover/cost accounting.

THE EXACT CONTROL BATTERY (all primitives already exist):
1. vs BUY-AND-HOLD: evaluate() on the overlay equity curve -> excess return, beats_sharpe, beats_sortino, beats_return, maxDD reduction. The CLAIM is DD reduction + Sortino, NOT total return, so judge on Sortino/Calmar/maxDD vs 100%-SPY, and report return give-up (the cost of insurance) honestly.
2. BETA DECOMPOSITION: beta_alpha(overlay_eq, SPY_eq). A defensive overlay should show beta<1 with alpha~0 — the test is whether risk-adjusted (Sortino/Calmar) improves AFTER netting beta, i.e. is there real timing skill or is it just a static beta<1 (which you get free by holding 0.8*SPY+0.2*cash with no signal). CRITICAL placebo: compare against a STATIC 0.85-beta sleeve (constant w=0.85) — the overlay must beat the static de-risked book, else the skew signal adds nothing.
3. OUT-OF-SAMPLE: evaluate(oos_frac=0.3) IS vs OOS Sharpe/Sortino on the 2016-2026 series. Also a fresh-regime holdout: fit nothing on 2020/2022, then check the overlay caught those specific tail events without being tuned to them.
4. OUT-OF-REGIME / per-calendar-year: reuse the per-year Sharpe loop from scripts/audit_overfit.py — overlay must reduce DD in the tail years (2018-Q4, 2020-Mar, 2022) WITHOUT bleeding too much in the calm bull years (2017, 2019, 2021, 2023-24). Count: in how many of the ~3 stress episodes did it actually cut DD, and what did it cost in the ~7 calm years.
5. REALISTIC COST: cost_bps=2.0/leg already in backtest_seasonal; weekly rebalance + hysteresis keeps turnover low so cost is a minor factor (this is NOT a cost-wall case). No borrow needed (long-only / cash-park). If the vol-sleeve (UVXY/SVXY) variant is run, apply the existing tail-stress injection (scripts/test_shortvol_tail_stress.py pattern: inject a -40/-50/-70% single-day vol-ETF shock at the joint-worst day) as a hard veto.
6. DSR / deflation: deflated_sharpe_ratio + probabilistic_sharpe_ratio on the overlay's EXCESS-over-static-de-risked-book curve, at n_trials=100/200/400 (project's true search breadth), using the threshold-grid Sharpe variance (skew/kurt threshold sweep) for sharpe_variance — same recipe as audit_overfit.py.

VERDICT RULE: ship only if the overlay (a) cuts maxDD/raises Sortino vs 100%-SPY, AND (b) BEATS the static-beta placebo on Sortino/Calmar (proves the signal not just lower exposure), AND (c) the DD-reduction shows up in 2018/2020/2022 specifically, AND (d) DSR survives ~200 trials on the excess-over-placebo curve. With only ~3 tail episodes in 2016-2026, treat as an uncorrelated risk-reducing OVERLAY leg for the combiner (like seasonality), never a standalone market-beater.
test_now.predicted_kill: Fails the static-beta placebo (control #2): the entire DD-reduction is just lower average equity exposure (holding ~0.85*SPY+cash), not skewness-timing skill. Rolling skew/kurt are LAGGING — by the time 60-day skew<-0.3 the crash has largely happened, so the overlay de-risks AFTER the drawdown and then sits in cash through the V-shaped recovery (2020, late-2018), giving up more upside than the DD it saved. Net: it beats unhedged SPY on Sortino only because a constant beta<1 sleeve does too — the signal adds no alpha over a no-signal static de-risk, and with just ~3 tail episodes in 2016-2026 the sample is far too thin to clear DSR at 200 trials. (Secondary kill if vol-ETF variant: UVXY/SVXY carry/decay + the existing tail-stress veto.)
test_now.objection: Rolling 60-day skew/kurt are LAGGING by construction: by the time trailing skew<-0.3 the left tail is already inside the window, so the overlay de-risks AFTER the drawdown — and the drawdown-gate only arms once already -10% down — then parks in cash through the V-recovery (2020, late-2018), giving up more upside than the DD it saves. Any DD reduction is mechanically reproducible by a no-signal static 0.85-beta sleeve, so the skew signal adds no risk-adjusted timing skill over constant de-risking. With only ~3 tail episodes in 2016-2026, the effective sample is far too thin to clear the static-beta placebo on Sortino/Calmar or to survive DSR at ~200 trials.

test_now.name: Seasoned Equity Offering (SEO) Announcement Reversal
test_now.source: Wall Street Research / Myers-Majluf Signaling; ScienceDirect
test_now.signal_rule: Event-driven: on announcement date of SEO (8-K, 424B5 / DEFM14A), stock falls 2–3% on average. Short at announcement or next open. Exit 20–40 days post-announcement (when market digests dilution signal). Pair with: only short if stock has <5% short interest (to avoid expensive borrow).
data_needs: SEC 8-K, 424B5 announcement filings (SEC EDGAR free, ~1-day lag)
test_now.claimed: −2 to −3% announcement effect is robust (80+ papers document; Myers-Majluf classic). Long-term (1-year) reversal possible if dilution is not offset by capex returns. Sharpe on short-term (20–40d) strategy estimated ~0.35–0.55.
test_now.primitive: Reuse alpca/backtest/pead.py + ear_pead.py (event-driven short, adverse_borrow model, beta-hedge), evaluation.py (buy_and_hold, beta_alpha, deflated/probabilistic_sharpe, oos), and the test_ear_pead_holdout.py fresh-symbol/per-year battery — change only the signal (fixed-short, direction not surprise-ranked) and add ONE new piece of code: an EDGAR full-text-search 424B5/8-K offering-date fetcher (efts.sec.gov + data.sec.gov/submissions, same UA pattern as scripts/download_fundamentals_edgar.py). No new backtest engine, no external drive.
test_now.test_plan:
REUSE the PEAD/EAR-PEAD event-driven machinery wholesale — this is structurally the same trade (event-dated single-name short held 20-40d), so build it as a thin variant, not from scratch.

DATA & CONSTRUCTION:
1) Universe (small, SIP-fetchable, NO external drive): ~25-40 large/mid-cap US names ($1B+) that have historically done dilutive raises and are GC-borrowable, e.g. biotech/growth serial issuers (MRNA, BYND, PLUG, RUN, CVNA, AMC, RIVN, LCID, SPCE, NKLA, COIN, MARA, RIOT, OPEN, AFRM, UPST, CHPT, FUBO, SOFI, DKNG) plus a few mega-cap controls. Fetch 2016-2026 daily SIP bars via the existing Alpaca bar fetcher (same loader pattern as test_ear_pead.py: cache/{sym}_1day_bars.jsonl), plus SPY for the hedge/benchmark.
2) Event source (the ONLY new code): SEC EDGAR full-text search (efts.sec.gov/LATEST/search-index?forms=424B5 and the companyfacts/submissions filing index at data.sec.gov/submissions/CIK{cik}.json — FREE, no quota, the same UA-header pattern as scripts/download_fundamentals_edgar.py). Pull, per ticker, the list of 424B5 (prospectus supplement = the priced offering) and 8-K offering-announcement filing DATES 2016-2026. Use the FILING acceptance date (public timestamp), enter at NEXT open (no look-ahead — same skip_days=1 convention as pead.py line 134-140). Emit events_by_sym[sym] = [{date: epoch, surprise_pct: -1 sentinel (direction is fixed-short), borrow_field: market_cap_bucket}].
3) Backtest: call backtest_pead / backtest_ear_pead in three modes exactly as test_ear_pead.py does — (a) naive single-name short, (b) beta_hedged (short name, long SPY by trailing-126d beta = the cheap-GC fix that rescued EAR-PEAD), (c) the raw -2-3% announcement-day capture vs the 20-40d drift, to separate the one-day signaling jump from the holdable drift. Sweep hold in {20,30,40}, entry filter SI<5% as the strategy specifies.

EXACT CONTROLS (the standing battery):
- vs BUY-AND-HOLD: long-leg/hedge must beat SPY B&H (buy_and_hold in evaluation.py) or it is beta, not alpha.
- BETA-DECOMPOSITION: beta_alpha() on the short+hedge equity vs SPY — confirm residual alpha after removing index exposure (SEO shorts are concentrated in high-beta growth names; a "short" basket can secretly be a short-beta bet that only worked because 2022 fell).
- ADVERSE-SELECTION BORROW (the kill control): run pead.py's adverse_borrow={base,special,sat,no_locate} — the names that raise equity when distressed are exactly the crowded/special/no-locate shorts. This is the model that killed surprise-PEAD (DSR 0.92->0.58) and SI-tilt.
- REALISTIC COST: cost_bps=2.0/leg + daily borrow drag on short notional.
- OUT-OF-SAMPLE: 70/30 time split (oos() helper).
- OUT-OF-REGIME: per-year Sharpe 2016-2026 (per_year() in holdout script) — SEO shorts pay in bear/dilution-fear years (2018Q4, 2022) and bleed in raging bull/squeeze years (2020-21 meme era, 2023-24). Require positive in a MAJORITY of years, not just 2022.
- FRESH-SYMBOL HOLDOUT (the definitive test, mode A of test_ear_pead_holdout.py): freeze params, split universe into disjoint TRAIN vs HOLDOUT name sets, require HOLDOUT Sharpe >0.3 and |TRAIN-HOLDOUT|<0.45. This is what killed EAR-PEAD after it passed everything else.
- DSR: deflated_sharpe_ratio over the hold/threshold sweep (~15-37 trials) — require DSR>0.9 on the SURVIVING (post-adverse-borrow) curve, not the gross one.
- SHUFFLE PLACEBO: randomize the event dates within each symbol's window; the real-date Sharpe must clear the placebo distribution (rules out "shorting these volatile names on any random day in 2022 worked").
test_now.predicted_kill: ADVERSE-SELECTION BORROW + SHORT-LEG-IS-A-LOSER, same wall that killed surprise-PEAD and SI-tilt. The -2-3% effect is a single-DAY announcement gap you cannot capture as a price-taker (you enter at next open, after the drop is already in), and the 20-40d holdable residual is thin. The names worth shorting on dilution (distressed growth/biotech) are precisely the special/no-locate/squeeze-prone borrows; the <5% SI filter shrinks the universe to names where the edge is weakest. After realistic borrow + 2bps/leg, the standalone short leg goes negative, and the beta_hedged version that survives will be carried by the long-SPY hedge (i.e. beta, not alpha) — caught by beta-decomposition. Even if it survives in-sample, the fresh-symbol + out-of-regime holdouts (positive only in down-years like 2022) will expose it as a short-beta timing bet, exactly as EAR-PEAD failed TRAIN +0.68 vs HOLDOUT -0.52.
test_now.objection: It is the 4th structurally-identical event-driven single-name SHORT (after surprise-PEAD, EAR-PEAD short leg, SI-tilt), and all three died to the SAME wall: adverse-selection borrow + short-leg-is-a-standalone-loser + fresh-symbol/out-of-regime holdout. The economics are actually worse here: the documented -2-3% effect is a ONE-DAY announcement GAP that a price-taker cannot capture (you enter at next open AFTER the 424B5/8-K already printed the drop), the holdable 20-40d residual is thin, the <5% SI entry filter deliberately selects the names where the dilution-short edge is weakest (easy-borrow = uncrowded = least mispriced), and the names actually worth shorting on distressed dilution (PLUG/MRNA/RIOT-type serial issuers) are precisely the special/no-locate/squeeze-prone borrows the adverse_borrow model drops. Any beta_hedged version that survives will be carried by the long-SPY leg = a short-beta timing bet that only pays in down years (2018Q4, 2022) and bleeds in 2020-21 squeeze/2023-24 bull — which beta-decomposition and per-year holdout will expose, exactly as EAR-PEAD failed TRAIN +0.68 vs HOLDOUT -0.52.
